conflicts.detail: Contact Address appears as TWO V4 table rows sharing one anchor #contact_address: one consumed from gov.identity (Mandatory '-', Source gov.identity, 'if more than one address exists the user must select one before continuing') and one user-created via Standard address block (Mandatory to proceed). How the two variants interact is unspecified. Modelled as a single contactAddress obligation (select-or-create) with fieldGroupRef address.

Stale bracketed counts in V4 Animal Identifiers commodity lists: 'Passport identifier commodities (6)' actually lists 9 entries (adds 0102 - Cattle and 01061900 - Cats/Dogs/Ferrets to the equine entries); 'Tattoo identifier commodities (2)' lists 5 entries (Cats, Dogs, Ferrets, 0103 - Pigs, 0102 - Bovine). Ear tag (4) matches. Spec adopts the bullet lists, not the bracketed counts.
conflicts.resolution: The commodity lists are truth; the bracketed counts are stale. Raise the count/list mismatch with the PO as a V4 page defect.

conflicts.detail: Structural disagreement on commodity hierarchy: the skeleton stores commodityComplement as a single-element array (one commodity line only) and hangs per-SPECIES dynamic inputs off it (noOfAnimals-{species}, noOfPackages-{species}, earTag-{species}, passport-{species}) plus derived totalNoOfAnimals/totalNoOfPackages subtotals. V4's data-level model is 1..N commodity lines per notification with quantity/packages at commodity-line level and identifiers at unit level (0..N unit records per line). Spec follows V4: commodityLines collection with a nested unit-level animalIdentifiers collection; the skeleton's derived subtotals are not modelled as obligations.
conflicts.resolution: V4 hierarchy stands: commodityLines 1..N with nested unit records; skeleton single-line commodityComplement and per-species dynamic inputs are implementation shortfalls, not requirements. PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by c-030 (Sam — Figma conflict walk 2026-07-13): per-species quantities are now a requirement adopted from the design, not a skeleton shortfall; the 1..N commodity-lines hierarchy itself stands.

conflicts.detail: Accompanying documents never reach the notification payload in the skeleton: buildNotificationPayload (src/server/common/clients/notification-client.js:108) has no documents section - files and metadata go to a separate document service via document-client keyed by uploadId, tied to the journey only by the session documents array. V4 defines the Accompanying Document block as notification-level data. Spec models a notification-level documents collection; how it reaches the payload is unresolved.
conflicts.resolution: Intended design, not a bug: documents persist in a separate Mongo collection and are foreign-keyed into the notification (by uploadId), not embedded in the payload. The spec's documents collection stands as journey-level obligations; persistence is by reference.

conflicts.detail: Dead address spokes: the skeleton addresses hub renders Add/Change links to /place-of-origin/select, /consignees/select and /importers/select but no such routes are registered, and session keys placeOfOrigin, consignee and importer are read (src/server/addresses/controller.js:69-75) but never written. V4 makes all three Mandatory to submit. Spec keeps all three obligations and invents provisional select pages for them (provisionalCopy=true).
conflicts.resolution: V4 party fields (place of origin, consignee, importer) are requirements; the skeleton's dead spokes are implementation gaps. Provisional select pages stand.

conflicts.detail: Validation coverage is uneven in the skeleton: only origin, cph-number, port-of-entry, accompanying-documents and declaration have Joi schemas (plus controller checks on consignment contact select); /commodities, /commodities/select, /import-reason, /commodities/details, /commodities/identification, /additional-details, /addresses and /transporters accept any payload with no server-side validation. V4 marks most of these fields Mandatory to proceed/submit. Spec adopts the V4 mandates.
conflicts.resolution: Skeleton validation gaps are not requirements; V4 mandates and formats win everywhere the skeleton lacks them.

conflicts.detail: CPH format disagreement: skeleton requires exactly 9 digits (^\d+$, length 9, inputmode numeric); V4 allows up to 11 characters including slashes ('123456789 or 123/456/789'). V4 wins: string max 11.
conflicts.resolution: V4 CPH format wins: string max 11 with examples 123456789 and 123/456/789; the skeleton's exactly-9-digits rule is not a requirement.

conflicts.detail: Reason for import value-set disagreement: skeleton offers 2 radios (internalMarket, reEntry); V4 defines 5 values (Internal market, Transhipment or onward travel, Transit, Re-entry, Temporary admission horses - the last carrying an unresolved inline comment). V4 wins.
conflicts.resolution: V4 five-value reason-for-import enum wins over the skeleton's two radios.

conflicts.detail: Animals certified for value-set disagreement: skeleton offers 3 radios (approvedBodies, breedingAndOrProduction, slaughter); V4 defines a 16-value single-select. V4 wins. Also PO-pending: APHA intend to make the field conditional for some commodities.
conflicts.resolution: V4 animals-certified-for value set wins over the skeleton's three radios.

conflicts.detail: Document field disagreements: skeleton document type enum has 2 values vs V4's 14; skeleton documentReference is optional with max 100 vs V4 All-or-nothing with max 58; V4 models Attachment Type as a user-selected enum of extensions (PDF/DOC/DOCX/JPG/JPEG/PNG/XLS/XLSX) while the skeleton derives it from an actual file upload (10 MB cap, virus scan, max 10 documents); skeleton requires date of issue only when a file is attached vs V4 All-or-nothing. V4 wins on data; skeleton upload mechanics noted on the collection.
conflicts.resolution: V4 document rules win: 14 document types and V4 reference/date rules; the skeleton's two-type enum is not a requirement. PARTIALLY REVERSED by c-034 (Sam — Figma conflict walk 2026-07-13): the skeleton's real upload mechanics return (cdp-uploader leg, virus scan, REJECTED blocking) and accompanyingDocumentAttachmentType is derived from the uploaded file rather than user-selected; c-010's data-rules-win spirit is kept for the payload shape.

conflicts.detail: Arrival date semantics disagree: V4 defines 'Arrival date at Port' (valid DD/MM/YYYY); the skeleton page asks 'When will the consignment arrive at its final destination?' (transport.arrivalDate) and its date validation has no completeness or real-date check (unlike issueDate). V4 wording and validation win.
conflicts.resolution: V4 'Arrival date at Port' semantics win; the skeleton page's arrival-at-destination copy is reconciled when the page is built.

conflicts.detail: Skeleton collects only a subset of the V4 fields. Missing entirely: region of origin code input, purpose in internal market, means of transport, transport identification, transport document reference, transited countries, transporter type split (commercial vs private), per-animal permanent address, and any display of Responsible Person for Load. Spec keeps the V4 obligations and invents provisional pages (provisionalCopy=true) where the skeleton has no collection point.
conflicts.resolution: The V4 field set is the requirement; fields the skeleton never collects stay in the spec and get pages.

conflicts.detail: descoped: Arrival time at port - de-scoped by Monica.Rivera on 14 May 2026. Not modelled as an obligation.
conflicts.resolution: Descoped by the PO (14 May 2026); correctly excluded from the spec.

conflicts.detail: descoped: Journey Log - de-scoped by Monica.Rivera on 14 May 2026. Not modelled as an obligation (Journey log survives only as a value in the Accompanying Document Type enum).
conflicts.resolution: Descoped by the PO (14 May 2026); correctly excluded from the spec.

conflicts.detail: descoped: Transport after BCP - de-scoped by Monica.Rivera on 14 May 2026. Not modelled as an obligation.
conflicts.resolution: Descoped by the PO (14 May 2026); correctly excluded from the spec.

conflicts.detail: descoped: Animal Identifier - Wing Ring - de-scoped by Monica.Rivera on 29 May 2026. Not modelled as an obligation.
conflicts.resolution: Descoped by the PO (29 May 2026); correctly excluded from the spec.

conflicts.detail: Wipe-vs-retain disagreement: the skeleton carries previously saved per-species noOfAnimals/noOfPackages/earTag/passport values over when species are reselected (retain), while the IxD canvas steer is 'delete conditional data on change of determining condition'. Spec defaults wipeOnExit=true on all conditional obligations; where retain is wanted this must be resolved at a gate session.
conflicts.resolution: Wipe on exit everywhere, confirmed: data whose determining condition flips out of scope is destroyed, not hidden. The skeleton's retained per-species values and the v4-model branch's retained regionCode are not requirements.

conflicts.detail: Option-data disagreement: skeleton commodity list is hardcoded (Cow/Fish/Cat/Dog), port list is hardcoded (ABERDEEN/EAST MIDLANDS AIRPORT/EDINBURGH), species/types come from mock-species.json and consignors/destinations/contacts/transporters from mock JSON; V4 sources commodities, types, species, ports and countries from named MDM lists (commodity search by common name, species and commodity code). Only the skeleton origin country list uses a real reference-data client. V4/MDM wins.
conflicts.resolution: MDM reference data wins: option lists (commodities, ports, countries, species) come from MDM-backed reference data, not the skeleton's hardcoded mocks. Spec optionsRef markers stand as the swap points.

conflicts.detail: Skeleton flow ambiguities around the addresses hub: cph-number is presented as a hub spoke (Add/Change link, back link to /addresses) but its POST jumps straight to /port-of-entry, short-circuiting the hub; the consignment contact page is registered inside the addresses plugin yet is only reached from the /transporters POST; and /import-reason (step 4) interleaves mid-commodity between /commodities/select (3) and /commodities/details (5). Spec groups pages by the skeleton's template page groups (About the consignment, Description of the goods, Transport) rather than the raw interleaved order.
conflicts.resolution: Navigation-layer, not model: hub/spoke wiring around addresses (CPH short-circuit, contact reached from transporters) is decided by flow.js + navigation when the addresses section is built. Obligations are unaffected.

conflicts.detail: Canvas open question: is shared data such as an address saved by copy, by reference, or both? The spec's address fieldGroup usages assume copy semantics by default (per the obligations-v2 model); the skeleton stores whole address objects picked from mock lists, which is copy-like but selected by reference index. Unresolved.
conflicts.resolution: Save by copy. Address (and similar shared data) is copied per usage, not shared by reference.

conflicts.detail: The V4 Number of packages commodity list (54) includes '0203 - Pig Meat under safeguard measure' - a product, not a live animal - and that entry carries an unresolved inline comment on '0203'. Several commodity codes also repeat across entries (e.g. 01061900 for Cats/Dogs/Ferrets/Rodents, 04071919/04071911 for several hatching-egg lines), so code alone does not identify a list entry.
conflicts.resolution: For this prototype, treat the V4 list as true and valid: 0203 Pig Meat under safeguard measure stays in the number-of-packages commodity list. Its unresolved inline comment remains a V4 page question for the PO, not a build blocker.

conflicts.detail: The declaration confirmation checkbox and submit action (DRAFT to SUBMITTED, no separate confirmation page) exist only in the skeleton; V4 has no declaration row. Kept as a skeleton-provenanced obligation since submission requires it.
conflicts.resolution: Declaration stays, sourced from the skeleton. End-of-journey shape confirmed: hub (task list) -> check your answers -> declaration -> submitted. SUPERSEDED in part by design evidence (A2/f-067/f-089/f-109, folded at M3-00): a separate confirmation page now exists (spec page confirmation, design 07-04) - declaration redirects there instead of back to /declaration. The conditional outstanding-items list on that page was rejected via c-029 (amend-and-resubmit guidance instead).

conflicts.detail: Mandate is two-level per gate ruling: mandatory-to-continue (blocks Save and Continue at point of display) vs mandatory-to-submit (blocks submission only). V4's 'Mandatory to submit' rows map to enforcedAt=submit; canvas says *some* questions are continue-level but neither source names which - every required obligation currently carries enforcedAt=submit|unspecified and no field is yet marked enforcedAt=continue. Needs per-field ruling (likely at M0/M1 page builds).
conflicts.resolution: countryOfOrigin and commoditySelection are enforcedAt=continue (cannot Save and Continue without them); every other required obligation is enforcedAt=submit. REAFFIRMED by c-029 (Sam — Figma conflict walk 2026-07-13): the design's submit-before-complete framing was rejected, so the two-level enforcedAt model stands unchanged (importType joins the continue level as the service entry filter).

conflicts.detail: V4 defect: the example value Imports456_GB contains an underscore that the stated alphanumeric pattern rejects. Prototype keeps the alphanumeric pattern and an underscore-free fixture; raise with the PO.

V4 note says region code is displayed/stored with an ISO country prefix derived from Country of Origin. M0 implements a plain 5-char input (hint: For example, FR-75) with no derived-prefix logic. Decide at a design gate whether auto-prefix behaviour is required.
conflicts.resolution: Plain input stands for the prototype; ISO-prefix derivation can be a later controller improvement, not a model concern.

conflicts.detail: V4's 'Optional - All-or-nothing' banner has no bite at the submit gate as modelled: statusOf treats a section whose only obligation is an optional collection as vacuously Fulfilled, so a half-completed document entry never blocks submission. collectionComplete models per-entry completeness but nothing consumes it for un-required collections. Design-gate call: extend the readiness rule to consume entryComplete for started entries, or accept the derived behaviour for the prototype.

Collection-level conditionality gap (Sam, M1 build): animalIdentifiers is modelled activatedBy:null + requiredAtLeastOne, i.e. EVERY commodity line needs >=1 identifier. But CPH is conditional on 19 named commodities, and V4's 'Passport identifier commodities'/'Tattoo identifier commodities' lists imply identifiers are commodity-conditional (some commodity types may need none). The per-identifier-type gating is captured as cross-frame modelGap on the item fields, but the COLLECTION lacks a commodity-level activatedBy. Re-check V4 at the M2 cross-frame model-extension gate: does animalIdentifiers need a collection-level activatedBy (which commodities require identifiers at all)?
conflicts.resolution: V4 re-read: collection is ALWAYS present (no collection-level activatedBy). Banner 'Mandatory to Submit - At least one … per animal' is universal; passport/tattoo/ear-tag/horse-name lists gate only the identifier-TYPE fields (frame:enclosing on species), and free-text fallback fields (Identification details / Description, 'shown when commodity has no specific identifier type') guarantee poultry/eggs etc. always satisfy 'at least one'. Spec's activatedBy:null on the collection is CORRECT — keep requiredAtLeastOne + sibling-at-least-one; gate only the type fields.
conflicts.resolvedBy: Claude (V4 re-read) — M2, on Sam's standing crack-on authority

conflicts.detail: PO ruling needed (surfaced by inc-031). The two free-text fallback identifier fields are 'shown when the commodity has NO specific identifier type' — a NEGATED cross-frame condition with no explicit commodity list, which the frame:enclosing/anyItem vocabulary cannot express (no includes list to gate on). inc-035 will render them ALWAYS-in-scope within a unit as the conservative prototype choice (slightly over-shows when a typed identifier applies, but 'at least one' still holds). Real options for later: (a) extend vocabulary with negated cross-frame gating, or (b) derive the fallback's negated set from the union of the typed-identifier lists. Also raise the V4 internal contradictions: per-field Mandatory column says 'Optional' while the block banner says mandatory-at-least-one; the field->type mapping is 'pending review by Monica' (not PO-signed-off); poultry/eggs identifier type is inferred from the fallback, not stated.
conflicts.resolution: DIRECTION AGREED (fields real, over-show is a model miss) — not fully closed: fallback survival is pending Monica's review, confirm with PO. The two free-text fallback fields are genuine V4 rows (bees/poultry-style commodities; examples 'Hive mark HM-2026-004', 'Worker bees in sealed transport hive') though the condition is unsigned ('pending review by Monica'). The always-in-scope rendering that lets free-text satisfy at-least-one for typed commodities is a prototype artifact of the missing negation vocabulary — fix via increment M3-0A (negated cross-frame gating: derive the complement of the union of the typed-identifier includes lists, or first-class negation vocabulary), a MODEL_EXTENDER pre-requisite for the design update; wipeOnExit keeps stale values from satisfying the group. Fallbacks then appear only for commodities with no typed identifier (V4's bees/poultry intent). IMPLEMENTED at inc-040 (M3-0A): activatedBy gained the complement-by-reference notInUnionOf form (Sam's D1(a) ruling) — both fallback fields gate on the enclosing commoditySelection being in NONE of the typed-identifier obligations' includes lists, with the union derived by the engine at runtime; see DESIGN-DELTA #7.
conflicts.resolvedBy: Sam — Figma conflict walk 2026-07-13 (direction only; final closure awaits Monica's review)

conflicts.detail: Submission model: design supports submit-before-complete (enabled Review-and-submit with 6 tasks To do; confirmation page lists documents + animal identifiers as post-submission to-dos 'before arrival at the port of entry') vs spec/prototype full-completion gate (readyForCheckYourAnswers, animalIdentifiers requiredAtLeastOne enforcedAt=submit). If design wins: new enforcement point (e.g. enforcedAt arrival), review-gate exclusions, post-submit completion loop via the dashboard. This decides the gating model. Findings f-022, f-034, f-068, f-097; reopens c-023, touches c-026.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - spec/prototype as-is wins. Submit gate stays: documents optional (mandate {}), animalIdentifiers requiredAtLeastOne enforcedAt=submit - at least one identifier record per commodity line, each record satisfying the identifier group; 1 record for 100 animals passes; no cardinality link to numberOfAnimalsQuantity (the c-031 count-cap/per-species-floor ruling later refines this). Per-commodity-line scope confirmed. Design's always-active Review button (f-022) and submit-before-complete framing rejected; no new enforcedAt=arrival point. Post-submit model: AMEND-AND-RESUBMIT - submit, later reopen from the dashboard, amend, resubmit; every resubmission passes the same submit gate. Not a post-submit top-up of a frozen record: amendment is a status transition back into an editable state, then declaration again. That is what the design's 'finish before arrival' confirmation copy actually means. Persistence note: the RECORDS-adapter freeze blocks writes to SUBMITTED - the amend action needs a sanctioned unfreeze transition, not a freeze bypass. Reaffirms c-023.

conflicts.detail: Commodity-line grain and batch selection: design's single search page with cross-code multi-select tokens, then one consolidated details page with PER-SPECIES quantity blocks, vs spec's per-line collection loop (one line per select/details pass). Per-species quantities revive the model c-003 ruled against. The 'design submits slugs not commodity codes' leg is STRUCK (Sam 2026-07-13): the export is a static mockup with no real form wiring; HTML names/values are artifacts, not a wire contract (see design-export-interpretive-rule); spec's commodity codes stand unopposed. Findings f-010, f-012, f-106; reopens c-003.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - design wins both remaining legs; slugs leg struck. (1) Batch selection adopted: one search page, multi-select species across commodity codes, several commodity lines created in one pass - replaces the one-line-at-a-time loop. (2) Per-species quantity grain adopted: number of animals / packages captured per species, not per commodity line. Rationale (Sam): easier to consolidate fine-grained counts UP (mappers sum to V4's commodity-level number) than to split a line-level count down later. V4's commodity-level numberOfAnimalsQuantity row is PO-pending anyway - raise the per-species grain with PO/Monica as V4 feedback, not a blocker. Partially supersedes c-003 (per-species quantities are now a requirement, not a skeleton shortfall; the 1..N commodity-lines hierarchy itself stands). Persistence: consolidation happens in Mapper A/B when building the notification payload - Mapper A stays skeleton-exact (sums to line level), Mapper B carries the per-species detail losslessly. MODEL_EXTENDER increment (batch-create + species-grain quantities); schedule after M3-0A.

conflicts.detail: Count-driven animal identifiers: design's fixed N-of-M loop tied to numberOfAnimalsQuantity ('Enter details for {species} N of M', no loop-exit affordance) vs spec's open 0..N collection with list+entry pages. If adopted: cardinality link + merged counter-driven loop. Findings f-014, f-031, f-035; touches c-027/c-028.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - design's count-driven loop adopted. (1) Cap: identifier records are capped by the declared animal count - say 10 animals, the loop stops at 10; counter UX 'Enter details for {species} N of M' with M = the per-species count from c-030's grain ruling. New model capability: a collection cardinality link (max = sibling count field) - MODEL_EXTENDER, schedule after M3-0A and with/after the commodities restructure. (2) Loop exit: the design's missing exit affordance is a design miss - adopt 'Save and finish' alongside 'Save and add another' (plus the standard task-page-exit-semantics exits). (3) Floor: moves to PER-SPECIES - at least one identifier record per species at submit, consistent with the per-species cards and the c-030 grain (refines c-029's per-commodity-line floor). (4) Count-drop semantics: block with an error, with a guided resolution path - never silently trim. Lowering a species' animal count below its existing identifier-record count is rejected with a GDS error summary naming the species ('you have M identifier records but entered N animals') linking straight to that species' identifier list.

conflicts.detail: Service-entry filter page 'What are you importing?' (live-animals / poao / hrfnao / plants): in the design, in neither spec nor V4; field name inconsistent in the export (importType vs importing). In or out of scope? Findings f-001, f-095.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - adopt the entry filter. 'What are you importing?' becomes the service front door: live-animals proceeds into the journey; POAO / HRFNAO / plants route to a 'not available in this prototype' holding page. Canonical field name set at M3-00: importType (the export's importType/importing inconsistency is an artifact per the interpretive rule - we choose). Spec page importTypeFilter added; the pre-hub linear opening run is c-040.

conflicts.detail: Transit/transhipment reveal fields: destination country (transhipment + transit) and exit BCP (transit) appear in the design with no V4 field and no spec obligation. Design ahead of V4, or unapproved scope? Also canonicalise the 'transshipment' spelling. Findings f-007, f-008, f-104.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - build now, PO flag. Transhipment/transit reveal fields (destinationCountry under Transhipment or onward travel and under Transit; exitBcp under Transit) are built as the design shows - conditional selects, wipeOnExit - and flagged to PO as a V4 amendment. If PO rejects, removal is one increment. Canonical spelling: transhipment (the export's transshipment is an artifact). Obligations added at M3-00, collected on the importReason page.

conflicts.detail: Documents: real upload vs attachment-type select. Design has drag-and-drop upload + virus-scan status and folds attachment type into file constraints; spec has the V4 attachment-type select and no upload leg. Limits also clash: design 50MB/15 files vs spec 10MB (CDP nginx cap)/10 documents. Design omits REJECTED-blocking and all-or-nothing error states. Findings f-059, f-060, f-091; reopens c-010.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - real upload, GDS widget, derived type. Real CDP-uploader upload leg adopted: reuse the skeleton's full-featured integration - the workspace stack already runs cdp-uploader as a docker image, so real mode uses the actual uploader locally; stub mode uses canned scan-state transitions (wired to the existing LIVE_ANIMALS_MODE stub|real switch). Attachment type is DERIVED from the uploaded file, not user-selected - spec amendment: accompanyingDocumentAttachmentType source flips User to System, the select goes away, the V4 payload field stays populated (raise the source flip with PO as V4 feedback). No drag-and-drop - not GDS; standard govukFileUpload (the design flourish is quietly ignored). Keep scan-status machinery: per-file status tags (design page 06-02 shows them), REJECTED-must-remove blocking, size/limit error states the design didn't draw. Limits: file size 50MB per the design - the earlier 10MB CDP-nginx-cap note is superseded (verify the stack's cdp-uploader/nginx config actually honours 50MB when building); max 10 documents (V4) stands, the design's 15 flagged to design. Partially reverses c-010 (upload mechanics return) while keeping its data-rules-win spirit for the payload shape.

conflicts.detail: Hub information architecture: design 'Overview' hub - 5 numbered groups over 10 page-level task rows, Draft tag, stat cards, no visible gating, always-enabled Review-and-submit button, Complete/To do tag vocabulary, back link + Return-to-dashboard - vs prototype's 7-section govukTaskList with GDS-canonical tags, Cannot-start-yet gating and a gated review row. Page-level rows change what sectionStatus is computed over. (The gating half of the question is c-029.) Findings f-020, f-021, f-023, f-026, f-096, f-098, f-111, f-030.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - design IA + chrome, GDS tags. Adopt the design's hub structure: 'Overview' page, 5 numbered groups over 10 page-level task rows (section-status computes per page-row - MODEL_EXTENDER), plus the design chrome (Draft/reference strip = M3-08, stat cards = M3-14, back link + Return to dashboard). KEEP the full GDS tag vocabulary (Completed / In progress / Not yet started / Cannot start yet / Optional) - the design's Complete/To do two-tag set cannot express gating or partial progress, both of which survive via c-029. Gating half settled by c-029: review row stays gated. Implemented at inc-061 (D11/D12/D13): status per PAGE-LEVEL task row (flow/task-rows.js; the what-are-you-importing and animal-identification rows split the commodityLines collection via engine collection-facet status parts, DESIGN-DELTA #13); readyForCheckYourAnswers re-expressed over the rows with submit-readiness proven unchanged; six rendered groups (the five answer groups plus the gated Check-and-submit row as group 6); h1 'Overview'; progress line dropped (D12); back link + Return-to-dashboard secondary replace breadcrumbs, no always-on review button (D13). The Arrival details row covers portOfEntry + transportDetails until the inc-065 page merge; the conditional Transit countries row renders only while transitedCountries is in scope; the identification row enters at the commodities list until inc-063's single identification surface.

conflicts.detail: Address-book picker + missing create-address form: design redesigns the four select spokes as a searchable paginated address-book table (40 records, radio-per-row, View details) with an 'Add a new address' button whose target form exists nowhere (not in design, spec or prototype) although V4's Standard Address Block implies user-created addresses. Data source, stable address IDs and selection-across-pagination are all open. Findings f-049, f-050, f-083; evidence on OPEN c-001.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - picker adopted + spec gains the create-address page. Address-book picker pattern adopted for the select spokes: search + pagination over a larger canned address book in stub data, radio-per-row, selection persisted server-side so pagination is no-JS safe, 'View details' per row. New spec page createAddress (added at M3-00): the shared V4 Standard Address Block form (9 fields) used by all spokes, closing the only modelled-nowhere hole and giving OPEN c-001's user-created contact-address variant its missing surface (design still owes the drawn page; not blocking). Programme context (Sam): at project level address management may become a whole separate team/service - the prototype treats addressing as a self-contained stubbed sub-service and does not over-invest in integration plumbing beyond the journey's needs.

conflicts.detail: typeSelection dropped: V4 marks type selection mandatory-to-proceed per commodity line; the design's commodity flow never collects it. Dropping needs PO sign-off. Findings f-011, f-082.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - drop typeSelection, pending PO sign-off. The c-030 search-first multi-select subsumes the type filter's purpose. Page dropped from the journey; the obligation stays in the spec marked pending-PO-drop until PO signs off dropping a V4 mandatory-to-proceed field.

conflicts.detail: Arrival/transport page architecture and widgets: design merges the two transport pages into one Arrival details page, uses a select for meansOfTransport (radios in spec), single-input JS date-picker for arrivalDateAtPort (GDS date-parts in spec), autocomplete add-another for transited countries (checkboxes in spec) and marks transit countries required-for-road/rail (Optional in spec - resolving the recorded V4 mandate tension toward required). Default until ruled: keep GDS date input and radios per the govuk-toolbox rule. Findings f-039, f-041, f-042, f-043, f-045, f-087, f-101; c-011 (copy part closed by A10).
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - merge adopted; GDS widgets; transit required. (1) Page merge adopted: one 'Arrival details' page collecting portOfEntry, arrivalDateAtPort, meansOfTransport, transportIdentification, transportDocumentReference (spec pages portOfEntry + transportDetails collapse; copy already Figma's via M3-04). Recorded as a merge-target note on both pages; page ids retained until the physical merge increment. (2) Widgets stay GDS: three-part date input (design's JS date-picker quietly ignored, same rule as drag-and-drop) and radios for meansOfTransport. (3) Transit countries: dedicated conditionally-routed page (M3-11, spec page transitCountries) stands; mandate resolves REQUIRED when meansOfTransport is Railway/Road Vehicle (design settles the recorded V4 tension toward required); capture as select rows progressively enhanced with the M3-20 accessible-autocomplete pattern, not a JS-only widget.

conflicts.detail: permanentAddress level: design shows Permanent address as a notification-level Roles-and-addresses row; spec/V4 make it a per-animal unit field for cats/dogs/ferrets only, with no per-animal entry designed anywhere. Findings f-071, f-079.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - V4 wins, no build. permanentAddress stays per-animal (cats/dogs/ferrets only), as the prototype already implements. The design's notification-level 'Roles and addresses' row is raised with the designer as an error (acceptable variant: read-only roll-up display of per-animal data on review).

conflicts.detail: Journey entry architecture: design opens with a pre-hub linear run (filter page -> draft created with visible reference -> About-the-consignment run -> hub as resting state); prototype is hub-first with enforcedAt-derived gating from a bare dashboard start. Where is the draft created, and is the opening run linear or hub-mediated? Finding f-094; depends on c-032, c-035.
conflicts.resolution: RESOLVED - adopt the pre-hub linear run. Journey opens linear: entry filter (importTypeFilter) -> origin (draft created + reference minted at the origin POST, as the spec already does) -> About-the-consignment run -> hub as the resting state thereafter. Matches the ixd-canvas 'linear followed by task list' behaviour.
